core_diagnosis.primary_failure: Model correctly caught total suppression but badly underestimated OKC margin separation because SAS offense cratered below rolling-window expectation.
core_diagnosis.first_half_context: Game was close at halftime, OKC 55-52, validating early script competitiveness before second-half break.
core_diagnosis.second_half_context: OKC won second half 64-46; Spurs turnovers and blocked attempts created one-way separation.
core_diagnosis.key_spurs_absence: Devin Vassell inactive with left adductor strain, reducing SAS spacing and secondary shot creation.
core_diagnosis.key_okc_absences: OKC played without Isaiah Hartenstein and Lu Dort but still won glass/paint/defense battle through Jaylin Williams, Chet Holmgren, Caruso, and SGA rim pressure.

phase_analysis.phase_1_opening: High-tension start. Spurs led briefly and generated early offensive rebounding through Kornet/Wemby, but SGA scored 13 in Q1 and OKC exited up 32-26.
phase_analysis.phase_2_second_quarter: Spurs stabilized and won Q2 26-23. Halftime 55-52 indicated the pregame close-spread script was live through 24 minutes.
phase_analysis.phase_3_third_quarter_break: Critical miss. OKC exploded 40-24 behind SGA/Jalen downhill creation, 69.6% FG in quarter, 22 paint points, and defensive blocks. This broke SAS +8.5 and forced total under to survive via SAS offensive collapse.
phase_analysis.phase_4_fourth_quarter: Game state became control/bleed. OKC led by 24 early Q4 and finished 119-98. Spurs never re-entered cover range.

model_patch_recommendations: Add THIRD_QUARTER_REVENGE_SURGE_GATE for elite home favorites after repeated matchup losses.
Add VASSELL_SPACING_ABSENCE_DOWNGRADE to SAS offensive mean, especially against elite rim protection/turnover defenses.
Reduce rolling-window spread weight when opponent enters revenge game with home court and prior 0-3 series record.
Create OKC_SMALLBALL_REBOUND_RESILIENCE tag: Hartenstein absence does not automatically create SAS rebounding edge when Jaylin/Chet/Caruso minutes expand.
Add RIM_EFFICIENCY_SUPPRESSION_FAILURE flag when Wemby/Kornet lineups allow opponent rim FG above 75%.
Separate total suppression from spread cover. Low total did not imply dog cover because one-sided efficiency gap widened.
